title: Lesson – Political and Legal Factors
description: Assessment of political systems, legal authority, employment protection, data protection, health and safety, consumer rights and simple contracts.
subject: Business and Technology

questions.question: A multinational company is planning to export goods and must comply with regulations issued by the World Trade Organisation. Which level of political influence does this represent?
questions.rationale: Rules issued by international institutions such as the WTO represent supranational legal authority affecting organisations across multiple countries.

questions.question: A national government introduces stricter environmental standards that increase compliance costs for manufacturing companies. How is the government influencing organisations in this situation?
options: Through government policy affecting industry behaviour
questions.answer: Through government policy affecting industry behaviour
questions.rationale: Government policy can influence business activities by setting priorities such as environmental protection which organisations must respond to.

questions.question: A city council requires all restaurants to obtain a hygiene certificate before operating. Which source of legal authority is responsible for this requirement?
questions.rationale: Local councils may introduce bye laws and regulations affecting businesses operating within their jurisdiction.

questions.question: A company dismisses an employee without providing the contractual notice period stated in the employment contract. Which legal issue could arise?
questions.rationale: Wrongful dismissal occurs when the employer breaches contractual terms such as failing to provide the required notice period.

questions.question: An employee resigns after the employer reduces their salary without agreement and fails to address workplace bullying. Which type of termination is most likely to be claimed?
questions.rationale: Constructive dismissal occurs when employees resign because the employer has breached the employment contract or created unacceptable working conditions.

questions.question: A manager dismisses an employee because the organisation no longer requires the role due to automation. Which reason for dismissal does this represent?
questions.rationale: Redundancy occurs when a role is no longer required because the organisation needs fewer employees.

questions.question: An organisation collects customer information but uses it only for the purpose originally communicated to customers. Which data protection principle is being followed?
questions.rationale: Data protection requires that personal data is used only for specified and explicit purposes.

questions.question: A company installs strong passwords and encryption to prevent unauthorised access to customer records. Which objective of data protection is primarily being addressed?
questions.rationale: Encryption and password controls are measures designed to ensure that personal data is protected from unauthorised access.

questions.question: An employee requests a copy of all personal information held about them by their employer. Which right is the employee exercising?
questions.rationale: Data protection regulations grant individuals the right to access the personal data held about them.

questions.question: A company installs CCTV cameras and access control systems in its data centre to prevent theft of equipment. Which risk is primarily being addressed?
questions.rationale: CCTV and access control are designed to reduce risks caused by unauthorised human access such as theft or vandalism.

questions.question: A firm stores backup copies of its critical data at a separate location outside the main office. What risk management strategy is this addressing?
options: Protection from total data loss due to disasters
questions.answer: Protection from total data loss due to disasters
questions.rationale: Off site backups ensure that data remains available even if the main system is destroyed by fire or other disasters.

questions.question: An organisation regularly inspects machinery and trains employees on correct operating procedures. Which legal responsibility is being fulfilled?
questions.rationale: Employers must ensure that equipment is safe and that employees are trained to use it properly.

questions.question: An employee notices exposed wiring in the workplace and immediately informs management. Which responsibility is the employee fulfilling?
options: Taking reasonable care for workplace safety
questions.answer: Taking reasonable care for workplace safety
questions.rationale: Employees must take reasonable care for their own safety and report hazards to the employer.

questions.question: A business sells a product that does not match the description provided in its advertisement. Which legal principle is most likely being breached?
options: Consumer protection relating to product description
questions.answer: Consumer protection relating to product description
questions.rationale: Consumer protection laws require that goods correspond with the description provided at the time of sale.

questions.question: A consulting firm completes a project for a client but fails to demonstrate reasonable professional care in delivering the service. Which consumer protection principle may be violated?
options: Services must be carried out with reasonable skill and care
Services must always be free of charge
questions.answer: Services must be carried out with reasonable skill and care
questions.rationale: Consumer law requires services to be delivered with reasonable skill and care.

questions.question: Two parties agree verbally on the sale of equipment without signing a written contract. Under contract law, which statement is correct?
options: The agreement is invalid because it is not written
The agreement may still be legally binding
The agreement is valid only if witnessed
The agreement becomes valid only after payment
questions.answer: The agreement may still be legally binding
questions.rationale: A simple contract can be legally enforceable even if it is not written.

questions.question: A contract between two businesses requires one party to deliver goods while the other pays for them. Which contract element does this exchange represent?
questions.rationale: Consideration refers to the value exchanged between parties in a contract.

questions.question: Two friends casually agree to go shopping together the next day but later change their plans. Why is this unlikely to be considered a legally binding contract?
options: There is no intention to create legal relations
The agreement involved more than two people
questions.answer: There is no intention to create legal relations
questions.rationale: Social agreements typically lack the intention to create legal relations required for enforceable contracts.

questions.question: A contract is signed with a person who is legally incapable of entering contracts due to age restrictions. Which requirement of a valid contract is missing?
questions.rationale: For a contract to be valid, each party must have the legal capacity to enter into the agreement.

questions.question: A business fails to comply with new legislation regulating workplace safety and receives a government penalty. Which consequence of legislation is demonstrated?
questions.rationale: Failure to comply with legislation may lead to penalties, fines or legal prosecution.

questions.question: A company encrypts sensitive client data before transmitting it online to prevent unauthorised reading of the information. Which threat is this control addressing?
questions.rationale: Encryption helps prevent confidential data from being intercepted or stolen during transmission.

questions.question: A multinational corporation must follow rules issued by the [dropdown_1], while also complying with regulations issued by the [dropdown_2] where it operates.
questions.rationale: Organisations may need to comply with supranational rules as well as national legislation.

questions.question: In contract formation, one party makes an [dropdown_1] and the other party provides [dropdown_2] to create a binding agreement.
questions.rationale: Agreement in contract law occurs when an offer is made and accepted.

questions.question: Health and safety laws place responsibility on both the [dropdown_1] to provide a safe environment and the [dropdown_2] to follow safety procedures.
questions.rationale: Both employers and employees share responsibilities for maintaining workplace health and safety.

questions.question: A legally enforceable agreement between two or more parties is known as a ______ contract.
questions.rationale: A simple contract refers to a legally binding agreement that does not require a specific form.

questions.question: When an employer breaches contractual terms during dismissal, it may lead to ______ dismissal.
questions.rationale: Wrongful dismissal occurs when an employer breaks the terms of the employment contract.

questions.question: The exchange of value between two parties in a contract is known as ______.
questions.rationale: Consideration is the value each party provides to the other within a contract.

questions.question: Which of the following are common areas covered by legislation affecting most organisations?
questions.rationale: Legislation commonly covers employment protection, data protection and workplace safety.

questions.question: Which of the following are examples of physical threats to information systems?
questions.rationale: Environmental events such as fire or flooding can physically damage information systems.

questions.question: Which rights may individuals have regarding their personal data?
questions.rationale: Data protection laws grant rights such as access, correction and deletion of personal data.

questions.question: Match the dismissal type with its description.

matchPairs.right: Employee resigns due to employer breach of contract
Employer cannot justify termination with valid reason
Employer breaks contractual terms when dismissing employee

questions.question: Match each level of legal authority with an example.
Match the workplace responsibility with the correct party.
Match the data protection principle with its meaning.

matchPairs.right: Information must be kept correct and updated
Data must be used only for specified purposes
Data must be protected from unauthorised access

questions.question: Match the consumer protection principle with the correct example.

matchPairs.right: Service completed within an acceptable timeframe
